source_pr.title: ddl/test: fix DATA RACE in TestResourceGroupBasic
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:

2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close #42119

Problem Summary:

### What is changed and how it works?

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors

- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: DATA RACE in `TestResourceGroupBasic`: a DDL callback updates `groupID` while the test concurrently reads it during assertions.
failure_signature: DATA RACE (race detector): concurrent read/write of `groupID` in `TestResourceGroupBasic`
root_cause_explanation: `TestResourceGroupBasic` registers an `onJobUpdated` callback to capture the created resource group's `SchemaID` into `groupID`, but the callback and the check/assertion code can run concurrently. Using a plain `int64` caused a data race when the callback wrote `groupID` while assertions read it. This PR fixes the race by switching `groupID` to `atomic.Int64` (using `Load()`/`Store()`), and also enables Bazel `race = "on"` for the test target to catch regressions.
fix_pattern: When callbacks/goroutines share state with the main test thread, use concurrency-safe primitives (e.g., `atomic.*` or a mutex) for shared variables; enable race detector for the test target to prevent regressions.

evidence: evidence: ddl/test: fix DATA RACE in TestResourceGroupBasic
